records.ety: [L. subsistere to stand still, stay, remain alive; sub under + sistere to stand, to cause to stand, from stare to stand: cf. F. subsister. See Stand.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Subsisted; p. pr. & vb. n. Subsisting.]

defs: To be; to have existence; to inhere.
quotes: And makes what happiness we justly call, Subsist not in the good of one, but all.

defs: To continue; to retain a certain state.
quotes: Firm we subsist, yet possible to swerve.

defs: To be maintained with food and clothing; to be supported; to live.
quotes: To subsist on other men's charity.

defs: To support with provisions; to feed; to maintain; as, to subsist one's family.
quotes: He laid waste the adjacent country in order to render it more difficult for the enemy to subsist their army.
